Great Spear of Dawn  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Piercing Atk Delay: 46
DMG: 53 Dmg Bonus: 158 AC: 25
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +10 STA: +10
SV MAGIC: +15
Effect: Dawncall (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 2.4 Size: TINY
Class: PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Great Spear of Dawn
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Piercing Atk Delay: 46
DMG: 53 Dmg Bonus: 158 AC: 25
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +10 STA: +10
SV MAGIC: +15
Effect: Dawncall (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 2.4 Size: TINY
Class: PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:2H Piercing
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:VaderFLAG
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 08:43:54
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

This item is found on creatures.

Temple of Veeshan
NPC Name
Sevalak
The Fabled Sevalak

Temple of Veeshan

Post Comment
appearance?
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2005 at 11:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591789738426
18 posts
ok so what does this actually look like? Someone said it looks like the primal spears, not like Narandis' Lance. But then someone else posted a supposed pic of it looking like NArandi's Lance.
▲ Page top
1h or 2h?
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2004 at 7:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592260908846
45 posts
Well personally i would suggest centi warspear, its about 50pp and at 20/30 its great for practicing your skill.

This weapon seems a bit slow for my liking, but its damage is good...and am i the only one who loves the 25AC it gives? I mean i like to do as much damage as i can but at the end of the day my job as pally tends to be tanking, so AC important to me, if i use 1h i get better ac but though i hit quicker i dont do as much damage due to poor damage bonus...not to mention the mobs beat the hell out of me with ripostes. So 2h means better dps and less ripostes on meso i like to use them even though i have to say goodbye to AC from a shield, so, a 2h that does big damage a proc AND gives me 25AC...surely a tanks dream?
▲ Page top
tried and true
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2003 at 1:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256764339
Scholar
316 posts
I've had this for a while, gave up my Black Bastardsword for it, and it rips up undead in Halls of Honor and Plane of Tactics. It's good in LDoN, also.

It's just way too slow. Sure on higher AC mobs (Luclin) it's nice because of the higher damage bonus, but a staple of both SKs and Pallies are their selfprocs and casting between swings, and even though this proc is nice and the swings are slow allowing for uninterrupted DPS due to casting, it *could* get faster. Needless to say I'm going to stick a damage modifier from LDoN on it just to see the big crits with it.

It's a big hitter, I like the slower, bigger hits, but when you miss a lot it really takes you down a few pegs DPS wise. It's just my "style" to have the bigger beefier hits (I love the tantor's tusk back when it was powerful seeming) but that's only until I land a goldenrod or elemental+ weapon.

The mob that drops it in NToV is the *GIMPIEST* of the triplets near the ring of vulak. It should be dead within 2 mins of engaging... in about 8 clearings of NToV that my guild has done, this weapon has dropped twice.
▲ Page top
RE: tried and true
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2003 at 10:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570795878
73 posts
/agreed
I ran this thing in lower guk then "upgraded" to it's one handed little brother after when I switched over to running with a serious shield (which I can't seem to stop now, going from the start point in the "shield of the protector", to "Tae EW", to "shield of the dawn" to "twice forged steel bulwark"). I want dis one back is where I'm thinking now though. Wit Dawncaller I'm doing an extra 30% damage from this proc, about 10dps or so. With this things slightly better dps on top of the proc I'd finaly match some of da ranja/warrior/monks I run with, and blow our wizzys away (average not max dps of course on the wizzy compare).

To get this kind of proc damage out of this or the one hander I've typically had chanty/shamy buffs to max my dex most of the time.

QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2002 at 3:35 AM Rating: Decent

44 posts
Hello guys I was just curious if anyone who actually had this weapon would post on its looks please. I was going to get one either way honestly but I have had a weapon called a Worn Shadin Naginta and I have seen the Lance of Thunder and it also looks just the same as that. It is an uber look like a long staff with a blade on the end, but I would prefer the Lance look if possible. I will be getting this weapon soon so I hope it looks good while on a horse hehe.

Zantetsukane Odinsfury
55th Level Teir'Dal Revenant
Rune of War

Hmm..no one has copy/pasted here yet...
QuoteReply
# Feb 11 2002 at 2:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754124041
133 posts
Dawncall
DoT for 750 Hit Points (HP) in 6 ticks


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Classes: None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Skill: Evocation
Allowable Targets: Undead

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Range to Target: 20 feet

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Resistance Check: Magic

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell Duration: 6 ticks (36 seconds)
Duration Formula: 3

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell cast on you: You stagger as the light of dawn washes over you.
Spell cast on someone: Soandso staggers as the light of dawn washes over it.
Spell fades: The light of dawn retreats.


This doesn't seem like a suitable proc for an SK...I mean, what SK worth his salt would call forth the Dawn?

Can Shadow Knights really use a 2 h P?
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2001 at 6:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764627525
5 posts
I've been having this discussion with my friends after seeing this item on this web site, and since WE ourselves can't get one, how do SK's use a 2HP when they only have 1HP that just doesn't make sense.
▲ Page top
RE: Can Shadow Knights really use a 2 h P?
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 4:52 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757351558
33 posts
Yes. The 2h piercing skill uses the regular 1h piercing skill to work off of. Examples are all of those velium lances that range from 30/40 to 32/36. They are all shadowknight useable and 2hand piercing.
▲ Page top
What to practice with
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 10:27 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Hello all, I am not here to argue with anyone here but to simply give some advice on how Paladins can work their peircing as quick as possible. I was farming FM for rares and killed a Goblin Trailblazer, he did not drop anything special, but did drop a pericing weapon that to my suprise Paladins can use. The stats kinda suck but does swing fast and can be weilded with a shield. 4/21 stam 4 is the stats, thanks and good luck. I am a 52 Paladin and am still using it to practice with, sometimes when im grouped =).
▲ Page top
RE: What to practice with
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2001 at 9:07 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
To answer your question there is a very easy way (not quickly) and I know alot of people will say EXPOIT. Go up to the 6th floor in the Tower of Frozen Shadow, spook a blue mob, he will go the the entry point. Proceed to hit him and you should be able to bring the skill up to a usable level within a few hours and get some exp to boot. I did the same thing with 2hb (0 to 170 or so in about 5 hours (along with half a level).
▲ Page top
RE: What to practice with
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2003 at 9:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570795878
73 posts
Wow, 2 years since the time of this post (how long before that was the 6th floor pathing this way?) and that is finally changed. Anyway just so ya all know, this trick doesn't work anymore with the pathing "fixes" that went in recently, the 6th floor is still a hell of a nice place to consistantly fight DB's in your High 40's but it aint the AE paradise it was.

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 05 2001 at 10:26 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hello all, I am not here to argue with anyone here but to simply give some advice on how Paladins can work their peircing as quick as possible. I was farming FM for rares and killed a Goblin Trailblazer, he did not drop anything special, but did drop a pericing weapon that to my suprise Paladins can use. The stats kinda suck but does swing fast and can be weilded with a shield. 4/21 stam 4 is the stats, thanks and good luck. I am a 52 Paladin and am still using it to practice with, sometimes when im grouped =).

#Anonymous, Posted: May 23 2001 at 6:29 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Not awesome at all... compare dual wielding jade maces (yes i know paladins cant do this) vs this spear (at lv 60)...

QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2001 at 1:51 PM Rating: Default

Anonymous
lol that guy thinks VI doesnt know what they are doing simply because they didnt allow his class to be god. WAH WAH WAH!

▲ Page top
RE: wtf are u on
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2001 at 1:48 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
KNOW YER ROLE AND SHUT YER HOLE!!

im an SK and i can humbly admit that it simply is NOT a hybrid's job too keep up in melee dmg with a monk or even a warrior. get a clue. if u ABSOLUTELY MUST do the most melee dmg than make a monk and quit yer bawlin'.

some ppl think they should be able to cast up a storm, stunning and healing AND keep up in dmg with warriors, rogues, and monks. well if that were the case the what would be the point of being a warrior rogue or monk? DUH!?!?!

accept yer positives and yer negatives and make the most of them. 2h weaps can be VERY powerful. their main purpose (especially on the monk 2hb's) is to give u an option if u happen to be taunting at an undesirable point in the fight, yet keeping dmg up (to some extent). also consider the makeup of your class. yes SKs do get lil better dmg weaps but as u can tell by our spells(DoTs, lifetap nukes, ATK buffs) we are mainly offensive, whereas pallies get lil better AC on their gear and spells like heal, AC buff, stun, HP buff. (sounds kinda defensive eh?)

the best players come form embracing their roles. i have grouped several awesome pallies who keep up their weaponry but dont dwell for a minute on the fact that they fall behind in melee just a bit. make the most of yer char and quit whining about yer weaknesses.
▲ Page top
hehe? wtf
QuoteReply

Anonymous
what the hell? a two handed tiny sized weapon =D it can fit in my pouches of quellious lol

# May 09 2001 at 11:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965790794
328 posts
This is most excellent. I always had faith that VI would bring meaning to giving PAL/SK the 2hP skill :) (yes I know it works off the 1hP skill, I'm not stupid).

Did anyone happen to notice that this awesome spear is TINY??? and weighs only 2.4 stone??? NICE! WTG VI!
▲ Page top
RE: Good LORD
QuoteReply
# May 10 2001 at 12:51 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592343248027
Scholar
48 posts
I admit that these 2hp weapons are real nice. This weapon is no exception. It's INSANE. My question is this. Is it worth using these new 2hp weapons? There are some damn nice 1 handed weapons out there. And you get to use a shield to bash and get a big boost of AC when using one handed weapons. Another thing is that in most groupes, Palies and SKs are used for meat shields, not DMG dealers. So tell me, would a Palie or SK really use this?

▲ Page top
RE: Good LORD
QuoteReply
# May 10 2001 at 2:10 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
At the level you need to be to get this, you would look a fool for having a shield unless it gave you Monster Stat boosts. AC means diddly to the high end mobs that drop this gear, you are much better off using 2 handed weapons. Plus there are some 2 handed weapons that allow you to bash still...I can't recall them, but I've heard about them (Firey Defender?).
▲ Page top
RE: Good LORD
QuoteReply
# May 10 2001 at 2:23 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593965942997
16 posts
The only 2 handed weapons i know of that allow you to still bash are epics unless your and Ogre, Troll or Barb who all have the slam ability which is useable at all times and for classes that get bash it works of that skill.

#Anonymous, Posted: May 09 2001 at 6:15 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) How are paladins able to use this without the piercing skill. Or has it been added to the skills they have?

# May 11 2001 at 3:55 AM Rating: Decent

What i want to know is where are the low level piercers for pallys or do thay have to get to 40-50 and then start using a 2hP with zero skill and bareky hit anything for ages ?

22 posts
Try bogling weps in echo caverns, they are all 1h and one is 8/29 one is 7/27? and the last is like 6/24. You could also try Needlite stinger off of the Needlite Queen also in echo. It is 7/24 and 2 disease dmg.

get a FS dagger, go to a zone where you can get xp( fi high keep or DL, depending on lvl) look for a group, say hi i wanna prac my piercing a bit,may i? and stab away with your 3 dam 19 delay weap) i am sure your skill will go up rather fast

#REDACTED, Posted: May 09 2001 at 4:27 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Try reading AND knowing what you're talking about. First of all, Paladins don't get duel wield, they get duel attack. Big difference. Second THIS item is a 2 hand piercing weapon, not a 1 hand slash item.
#REDACTED, Posted: May 09 2001 at 6:33 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) minor thing: double attack :)
RE: RTFQ!
QuoteReply
# May 11 2001 at 3:52 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756763344
View User Forum
239 posts
Other minor thing: Duel is usually defined as a single combat between two individuals, usually for the purpose of defending one's honor. Dual, however, means two-at-once. ;)

-Nastasja Wyldangyl
32nd season Knight of the Cathedral
Innoruuk
